items.prompt: multiplies and divides bounded rational numbers across fraction, decimal, signed, and rate-based representations
items.expected_evidence: multiply and divide rational quantities using scaling, unit-rate, or inverse structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner computes the result accurately while preserving scale and unit meaning.

items.prompt: explains how scaling, unit-rate, inverse, or sign structure justifies a rational-number product or quotient
items.expected_evidence: explain why the result is reasonable using unit, scale, benchmark, or sign language
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the operation using coherent multiplicative and sign structure.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to sign_rule_without_meaning.
items.expected_evidence: interpret products and quotients involving fractions, decimals, and signed values coherently
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how the peer produced a sign or scale result without structural justification.

items.prompt: succeeds across symbolic, table, verbal, and simple contextual rational-number multiplication and division cases
items.expected_evidence: connect symbolic work to rate, scaling, grouping, and bounded contextual situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves multiplicative rational-number meaning across multiple representations and contexts.
